metadata.title: Why Deleting the Cheapest Job Destroys the Most Expensive One
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Reallocation Engine candidate 11. Source chapter: books/the-reallocation-engine/chapters/01-the-fluency-trap.md (the 'Rung That Got Removed' section only — junior work manufactures senior judgment).
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper collage on real newsprint scan; desaturated plates; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines; hand-drawn annotation ring used once per graphic at most; a career ladder with a senior-judgment platform as the hero object
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, dusty navy = the pipeline / people / judgment being built, crimson = the cut rung / the drain / the empty platform, gold highlighter as the single editor's-pen voice

metadata.color_semantics: dusty navy #3D5A80 = the pipeline / climbers / judgment being built; crimson #BF3339 = the cut rung / the drain / the empty platform; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO wage-premium statistics (no 56% premium, no 'poach at a premium' figures), NO '90% musician' inversion material (§80 skipped), NO IBM detail beyond the ONE announcement (only 'IBM will triple US entry-level hiring, incl. software roles' + its one-line structural rationale — NOT the systems-judgment role-rewrite specifics), NO policy prescriptions (describe, don't prescribe). Hero: a ladder losing its bottom rung; climbers can't board; the senior platform drains on a delay (manim move: drain). Verified from ch.1 §68-82: judgment forms through junior grunt work ('the grunt work was the apprenticeship'); the rung AI executes best (boilerplate, cleanup, first drafts) is the exact rung where judgment was learned; cutting it is the rational short-term move; the drain shows up in 3-5 years with nothing connecting it to the cut; 'cut the bottom rung and you do not get cheaper seniors. You get no seniors'; Feb 2026 IBM (CHRO Nickle LaMoreaux) announced tripling US entry-level hiring including software developers.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B11: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Cutting entry-level jobs saves money this quarter, and quietly erases the senior experts of three years from now. And no dashboard connects the two events.

card.copy: Why deleting the cheapest job destroys the most expensive one

beats.new_visual_element: [TITLE] delete the cheapest job, destroy the priciest

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [TITLE] delete the cheapest job, destroy the priciest

beats.narration_text: On paper it's obvious. The junior role is the cheapest, and AI does that work instantly. So why keep paying for it? Cut it. The quarter looks great.
beats.scene_description: an executive at a dashboard pinned to the newsprint ground, a red line struck through an 'entry-level hiring' budget row, a green 'savings' arrow ticking up; slow push-in on the struck row
beats.image_prompt: printed screenshot of a corporate budget dashboard with a line item 'entry-level hiring' struck through in red and a green savings figure rising, an executive silhouette reviewing it, pinned like a clipping to aged newsprint, desaturated flat print reproduction, editorial collage
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] the budget cut — entry-level struck, savings up

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] the budget cut — entry-level struck, savings up

beats.narration_text: But that cheap bottom rung was never really about the work. It was an apprenticeship. Two years of grunt work is how judgment quietly gets built.

production_viz.label: the ladder to judgment
production_viz.mechanic: a navy career ladder; the bottom rung labelled 'junior work'; navy climber figures ascend; a top platform labelled 'senior judgment' with a couple of figures standing on it
production_viz.note: establish the hero — junior rung feeds the senior platform

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] the ladder — junior rung up to senior platform

beats.narration_text: The grind wasn't the inefficiency. It was the training. You built the eye by doing the boring work nobody senior wanted.

document.quote: The grunt work was the apprenticeship.

beats.scene_description: the core claim as a large serif quote card; gold highlighter sweeps 'apprenticeship' as it is spoken
beats.new_visual_element: [QUOTE] the grunt work was the apprenticeship

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [QUOTE] the grunt work was the apprenticeship

beats.narration_text: And here's the cruel part. The rung AI does best — boilerplate, cleanup, first drafts — is the exact rung where that judgment was learned.

production_viz.label: AI does best = where judgment is learned
production_viz.mechanic: the ladder returns; the bottom rung gets two overlapping tags landing on the SAME rung — a navy 'where judgment is learned' and a crimson 'what AI does best'; a crimson ring shows they are the same rung
production_viz.note: the overlap — the cruelty of the mechanism

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] both tags land on the same bottom rung

viz.label: the AI rung and the learning rung are one

beats.narration_text: So the rung gets cut. Instantly cheaper. But now no one can climb on, and the platform of seniors at the top slowly starts to empty.
beats.scene_description: the bottom rung is removed (a crimson gap); a climber figure at the base can't reach the next rung; up top, figures on the senior platform begin to fade out one by one — the drain begins

production_viz.label: cut the rung → the platform drains
production_viz.mechanic: the bottom rung vanishes leaving a crimson gap; a navy climber stalls at the base, unable to board; on the top platform, navy figures fade out one at a time; a crimson down-arrow on the platform marks the drain
production_viz.note: the drain move — the heart of the film

beats.new_visual_element: [COMPOSITE] rung cut, climber stalls, platform empties

beats.narration_text: Here's why nothing catches it. The savings land this quarter. The empty platform lands years later. No dashboard connects a cut that fast to a gap that slow.

production_viz.label: the cut is fast, the gap is slow
production_viz.mechanic: a timeline; a crimson 'savings' tick lands at 'this quarter'; far to the right a crimson 'no seniors' marker lands at '3-5 years'; a dashed link between them is drawn and then breaks (a broken chain icon)
production_viz.note: 3-5 year delay — verbatim; the reason no dashboard connects them

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] savings now vs empty platform in 3-5 years, link broken

beats.narration_text: And you can't buy your way out. Cut the bottom rung, and you don't get cheaper seniors. You get no seniors. The pipeline that made them is gone.

production_viz.label: not cheaper seniors — no seniors
production_viz.mechanic: the empty top platform; a 'hire a senior' arrow reaches up from below and finds nobody — it closes on empty; a crimson 'none' tag; the ladder below still shows the missing bottom rung
production_viz.note: 'you do not get cheaper seniors. You get no seniors.' — verbatim. No wage-premium/poach figures (card exclusion).

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] reach for a senior, find none

beats.narration_text: One company said it out loud. In 2026, IBM announced it would triple its entry-level hiring, including the software jobs we're told AI can do. Not sentiment. Structure.
beats.scene_description: the ladder's bottom rung is rebuilt navy and widened to three rungs (tripled); fresh navy climbers board; a slate card reads 'IBM · triple entry-level hiring · 2026'; label 'not sentiment — structure'

production_viz.label: IBM triples the bottom rung
production_viz.mechanic: the missing bottom rung is redrawn navy, then two more rungs stack beside it (tripled); navy climbers step on; a slate-teal entity card 'IBM — triple entry-level hiring, 2026'; serif label 'not sentiment — structure'
production_viz.note: the ONE IBM announcement only — no systems-judgment role-rewrite detail (card exclusion)

beats.new_visual_element: [COMPOSITE] IBM rebuilds and triples the bottom rung

beats.narration_text: The pipeline that makes senior judgment runs straight through junior work. Cut the cheapest rung, and years later the most expensive one is simply gone.
beats.scene_description: the full ladder with a navy flow arrow running from the bottom rung up to the senior platform; the editor's pen rings the bottom rung as the source; label 'the cheapest rung feeds the most expensive one'

production_viz.label: the cheapest rung feeds the priciest
production_viz.mechanic: the whole ladder; a navy upward flow arrow from bottom rung to top platform; a crimson HandRing lands around the bottom rung on 'cheapest rung'; label 'cut here, lose the top'
production_viz.note: the rule, restated on the hero object

annotation.target: the bottom rung (the source of the pipeline)

beats.new_visual_element: [COMPOSITE] flow from bottom rung to top; ring the source

beats.narration_text: The cheapest rung is where the expensive one gets made.

card.copy: The cheapest rung — where the expensive one is made.

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] endcard — the cheapest rung makes the expensive one

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] endcard — the cheapest rung makes the expensive one
